name: Leafcutter Ant
summary: The leafcutter ant is a fascinating insect famous for its farming skills. They cut leaves from trees and carry them back to their underground nests to grow...
metaTitle: Leafcutter Ant Facts | Habitat, Diet, Behavior & Photos
metaDescription: Leafcutter ant facts for kids with photos, tropical habitat, diet, behavior, and farming secrets on one easy-to-read page.
adaptations: ability to carry 50 times their weight
special pockets to carry starter fungus
funFacts: Leafcutter ants are the world's first farmers, growing their own fungus crops millions of years before humans existed!
A single leafcutter ant can carry a piece of leaf that is 50 times heavier than its own body.
Their massive underground colonies can contain up to 5 million ants and span over 30 feet wide.
The colony is divided into different sizes (castes), including tiny gardeners, leaf cutters, and giant soldiers.
When a new queen leaves to start a new nest, she carries a tiny bit of the mother colony's fungus in a special pocket.
They do not eat the leaves they cut; they use them as compost to feed and grow their underground fungus gardens.

faq.question: What is a leafcutter ant?
faq.answer: A leafcutter ant is a species of ant famous for cutting pieces of leaves and carrying them back to its nest. They are social insects that live in highly organized colonies.

faq.question: Do leafcutter ants eat leaves?
faq.answer: No, they do not eat the leaves. Instead, they chew the leaves into a paste and use it to grow a special type of fungus, which is the only food the colony eats.

faq.question: How much weight can a leafcutter ant carry?
faq.answer: A leafcutter ant can carry a leaf piece that is up to 50 times its own body weight. This is like a human carrying a car on their back!
